title: Revisit archival mechanism: epic-level atomic archive + reference integrity sweep
description:
Follow-up to the archival doc-reference integrity fix. Revisit the archival mechanism and UX holistically rather than only patching per-document archive.

Motivation: epic 25064508's archival left dangling references because archival is per-document and not reference-aware/transactional at the epic level. A retiring epic has many artifacts (plan, completion report, showcase, dogfood evidence) and many cross-issue references; doing this one file at a time is error-prone.

## Possible scope (to be refined)

- An epic-level `jit archive <container>` that atomically relocates ALL of a container's artifacts and re-links every reference across the graph in one transaction, aborting if any artifact is missing.
- A `jit doc verify` / integrity sweep surfacing dangling references before they reach `jit validate`.
- Clear UX for what "archive" means for a bracket (plan, breakdown, completion report) and how the planning node's plan reference is carried.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] A design note evaluating epic-level atomic archival vs the per-document model, with a recommendation.
- [ ] (If approved) an implementation that makes archiving a completed epic a single safe operation that never leaves dangling references.
